abstract.para: By default, logging into Cockpit as a regular user requires an existing $HOME directory. This requirement exists because the system defaults to supporting standard Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) mechanisms via PAM.

topic.para: If a user does not have a $HOME directory—which frequently occurs during the initial login of network users managed via SSSD—the login attempt fails. To allow users without home directories to log into Cockpit, override the default PAM configuration to disable the look-up for the second-factor authentication file:

important.para: By following this procedure you will not be able to use a second-factor authentication token via pam_oath.

step.para: Copy the system default Cockpit PAM configuration to the administrator override directory:
Open /etc/pam.d/cockpit in an editor and locate the line referencing pam_oath.so at the end of the file.
Comment out or remove the line entirely:
step.screen: &prompt.root; auth [user_unknown=ignore success=ok] pam_oath.so usersfile=${HOME}/.pam_oath_usersfile no_usersfile_okay window=20 digits=6
